title: The Grey Wolf
content:
'A fagot, a fagot, go fetch for the fire, son!'
        'O, Mother, the wolf looks in at the door!'
     'Cry Shoo! now, cry Shoo! thou fierce grey wolf fly, now;
        Haste thee away, he will fright thee no more.'

     'I ran, O, I ran, but the grey wolf ran faster,
        O, Mother, I cry in the air at thy door,
     Cry Shoo! now, cry Shoo! but his fangs were so cruel,
        Thy son (save his hatchet) thou'lt never see more.'
meaning:
About: Walter John de la Mare was an English poet, short story writer and novelist, best remembered for his works for children and "The Listeners".

He was born in Kent, and was educated at St Paul's Cathedral School.

His first book, Songs of Childhood, was published under the name Walter Ramal.

His 1921 novel, Memoirs of a Midget, won the James Tait Black Memorial Prize for fiction.
